result:
Now I have the context I need. Here is my full adversarial review.

---

```
RANGE: 130fe06..38953e3
FILES: 107 files changed

## Findings

### [SEVERITY: critical] bout-sweep refund reversal is not atomic - unconditional status reset
File: lib/bout-sweep.ts:126-132
Pattern: WD-LRT (Looks Right Trap)
What: When applyCreditDelta throws, the bout status is reset to 'running' with WHERE that only checks bout ID, not current status.
Why: A concurrent sweep that claimed the same bout between the refund failure and the reset would have its work undone. The unconditional UPDATE breaks the atomic claim guarantee established by the RETURNING pattern at line 62-74.
Fix: Change `.where(eq(bouts.id, bout.id))` to `.where(and(eq(bouts.id, bout.id), eq(bouts.status, 'error')))`.

### [SEVERITY: major] bout-sweep infinite retry loop on persistent refund failure
File: lib/bout-sweep.ts:107-147
Pattern: structural
What: When applyCreditDelta consistently throws (e.g., corrupted credit account), the bout is reset to 'running', the next sweep picks it up, fails again, resets again - indefinitely.
Why: No circuit breaker, retry counter, or escalation path. Each sweep invocation burns DB queries and log entries without progress. The bout never reaches a terminal state.
Fix: After N consecutive failures (trackable via metadata or a retry_count column), leave the bout in 'error' status and flag for manual review instead of resetting to 'running'.

### [SEVERITY: major] bout-sweep SELECT has no LIMIT clause
File: lib/bout-sweep.ts:43-51
Pattern: structural
What: The initial query `db.select().from(bouts).where(...)` loads all stuck bouts into memory without a LIMIT.
Why: A cascade failure (widespread serverless timeout) could leave thousands of bouts stuck. Loading them all into memory risks OOM in a serverless function, and sequential processing risks function timeout before completing any refunds.
Fix: Add `.limit(100)` or paginate. The endpoint is designed to be called repeatedly (cron), so bounded batches are safer.

### [SEVERITY: major] bout-validation.ts imports unused symbols from ai and presets
File: lib/bout-validation.ts:21-26
Pattern: WD-DC (Dead Code)
What: `getModel`, `getInputTokenBudget`, and `DEFAULT_AGENT_COLOR` are imported but never referenced in bout-validation.ts. They belong in bout-execution.ts.
Why: Dead imports increase bundle size and create false dependency signals. A developer reading this file assumes these are used in validation logic. TypeScript won't catch this since they're valid exports.
Fix: Remove the three unused imports from bout-validation.ts.

### [SEVERITY: major] billing.ts handleSubscriptionUpdated: old tier read is not transactional with tier write
File: lib/billing.ts:199-248
Pattern: structural (TOCTOU)
What: The old tier is read from the users table (line 212-217), then the new tier is written (line 221-230), then credits are granted based on the delta. These three operations are not in a transaction.
Why: Two concurrent subscription.updated events (e.g., rapid free->pass then pass->lab) can both read old tier as 'free', both grant full credits from free, missing the incremental pass->lab delta. The referenceId includes the old tier so idempotency guards don't prevent this - the two events have different referenceIds.
Fix: Wrap lines 212-260 in a db.transaction() to ensure the old tier read and the new tier write are atomic.

### [SEVERITY: minor] bout-execution.ts transcript truncation floor is 1 entry
File: lib/bout-execution.ts:612-620
Pattern: WD-PG (Paper Guardrail)
What: The while loop `transcriptForLog.length > 1` never reduces below 1 entry. A single transcript entry exceeding 100KB (e.g., very long LLM response) will still be logged to Sentry at full size.
Why: Sentry caps event payloads at ~200KB. A single 150KB transcript entry would exceed the Sentry limit and be silently dropped, defeating the recovery mechanism this code exists to provide.
Fix: After the loop, check if the remaining entry exceeds the budget. If so, truncate the text field of the last entry to fit, with a `[truncated]` marker.

### [SEVERITY: minor] bout-execution.ts completion retry uses fixed 500ms sleep
File: lib/bout-execution.ts:650
Pattern: structural
What: `await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 500));` adds 500ms to the error path unconditionally.
Why: In a serverless function approaching its timeout, this 500ms delay is wasted and could push the function past timeout before the retry attempt. The retry then fails with a timeout error instead of the DB error.
Fix: Consider exponential backoff starting at 100ms, or skip the delay entirely since the transient condition (e.g., Neon cold start) is often resolved by the time the JS event loop processes the next tick.

### [SEVERITY: minor] cache.ts - leaderboard data loses Date type fidelity through JSON round-trip
File: lib/cache.ts + lib/leaderboard.ts
Pattern: WD-SH (Semantic Hallucination)
What: LeaderboardData stored via cacheSet undergoes JSON.stringify/parse. Any Date objects in the data become ISO strings after deserialization.
Why: If LeaderboardData contains Date values (e.g., from `createdAt` columns), consumers receiving cached data will get strings where they expect Date objects, causing `.toISOString()` calls to throw.
Fix: Verify LeaderboardData contains no Date fields, or add reviver logic to cacheGet for known date fields.

## Anti-Pattern Checklist

| ID | Verdict |
|----|---------|
| right-answer-wrong-work | NOT FOUND |
| phantom-ledger | NOT FOUND - credit ledger entries match operations correctly |
| shadow-validation | NOT FOUND |
| error-string-archaeology | NOT FOUND |
| half-life-clock-skew | NOT FOUND - intro pool uses SQL EXTRACT EPOCH consistently |
| mock-castle | NOT FOUND - test mock scaffolding is proportional to assertions |
| phantom-tollbooth | NOT FOUND |
| schema-shadow | NOT FOUND |
| confessional-test | NOT FOUND |
| cost-margin-asymmetry | NOT FOUND |
| paper-guardrail | FOUND - transcript truncation floor (see finding above) |
| stale-reference-propagation | NOT FOUND |
| loom-speed | NOT FOUND |

## Summary

Findings: 8 (critical: 1, major: 4, minor: 3)
Verdict: PASS WITH FINDINGS
```
